apify_person_profile_items.description:
Every viral LinkedIn post is a lead list hiding in plain sight.
 
2,000 people commented. 500 reacted. They told you what they care about. They showed intent. Their exact words are sitting right there in the comment section.
 
And then nothing happens. You scroll through a few, maybe connect with two or three, and the rest disappear into the feed forever.
 
I've spent the last few months obsessing over one question: what happens when you treat LinkedIn engagement as a prospecting signal instead of a vanity metric?
 
Turns out, a lot. Commenters convert 4x better than random cold lists. People who ask questions in comments are the highest-intent leads you'll ever find. And the data sitting inside a single viral post, job titles, companies, industries, seniority, is richer than most paid databases.
 
So I built PostLeads.
 
Paste any LinkedIn post URL. Get a CSV of every commenter and reactor, enriched with 36 fields of profile data, company info, engagement context (including their exact comment text), and verified work emails.
 
No LinkedIn login. No Chrome extension. No subscription. CSV in your inbox in 5–15 minutes.
 
I write about intent-based outbound, LinkedIn engagement signals, and the shift from spray-and-pray to signal-based selling.
 
Try it → getpostleads.com

fields_of_study: Bachelor's degree, Chemical Engineeing with specialisation in Refining and Petrochemicals

featured.subtitle: getpostleads.com
featured.text: No LinkedIn login required | No chrome extensions
featured.title: Turn any LinkedIn post into a verified lead list

featured.text:
A single LinkedIn post generated 5,088 warm leads.
Not from ads. Not from a database. From the comment section.

Taylor Haren posted about replacing Clay with Claude Code. 5,088 people commented "CODE" because they want a better lead enrichment system.

Every one of them publicly revealed their name, job title, company, and exactly what they care about.
That's insane intent data. But LinkedIn gives you no way to export it, filter it, or use it.

So I built a tool that does.

It's called PostLeads. 

Paste any LinkedIn post URL → get every commenter and reactor exported to a CSV with enriched profiles and verified work emails.

I ran it on Taylor's post. 9 minutes later:
→ 5,088 leads exported → Full name, headline, job title, company, industry, location → 1,900+ verified work emails → The exact comment each person left → A flag on every commenter who asked a question (your hottest leads) → 36 data points per person

From one URL. In 9 minutes.

Now imagine sending a DM that says: "Saw your comment on Taylor's post about Clay alternatives, I actually built something that might help."

That's not cold outreach. That's a warm conversation starter with someone who just told you what they need.

The tool is live. getpostleads(dot)com (link in comments).

Starts at $5. No subscription. No LinkedIn login. No Chrome extension.
I put together a sample from this exact export, 50 rows with all 36 columns visible so you can see exactly what you get.

Comment “LEADS" and I'll send it to you.

Video walkthrough👇

apify_company_profile_items.name: Google

update_data.content:
When there was an opportunity to join the Cloud Business Intelligence team as a Looker Sales Lead in a new market, Rakhi made the move from India to our Melbourne Docklands office. 

Once she arrived, she found that the role was about much more than just numbers.  "Working at Google and representing Looker means more than just solving technical problems; it’s about the people. From critical customer issues to representing our tech at industry events, the energy is non-stop," she explains. “The most impactful conversations happen when we move past 'how the tool works' and start discussing how data can reshape a business model.”

To keep the creative momentum going after those big conversations, Rakhi relies on a workspace designed for spontaneous "aha!" moments. "You’ll find graffiti walls in meeting rooms, quirky Melbournian names for meeting rooms, large open space for events, table tennis/pool, photo booth and plenty of cozy breakout spaces with seating," she says about the office.

Today, Rakhi’s impact continues to grow beyond her day-to-day role. She leads initiatives for Women@Australia, and collaborates with Google Research on hearing health accessibility.

Interested in joining Rakhi and the Cloud team? Check out our open roles →https://goo.gle/4mdmcVz

media.alt: Rakhi smiling for a close-up selfie in front of a large, colorful Google "G" logo.
A close-up shot of a Google branded sneaker featuring the “Google” logo and brand colors on the heel.
Rakhi and a colleague sitting side-by-side at a office desk smiling to the camera.
Rakhi playfully posing on a bicycle positioned under a bright green beach umbrella inside a Google office space.
A cheerful selfie of Rakhi and a colleague standing in front of the iconic Google "G"

They demonstrate how hands-on tools like 3D physics sliders and custom animation timelines enable teams of designers and engineers to work together to explore new ideas.…more

PSA: You can change your Google account username without losing access to your entire account history. That way, you don’t have to make a new account to avoid using your Gmail address from years ago. 

Here’s how ⬇️…more

Tag your friend or co-worker who needs to hear this: If you’re a U.S. Google user, you can now change your account username for tools like Gmail, Photos, Drive and more — while keeping your emails, data and account history. Here’s what to know: 

1️⃣ You can choose any available @gmail.comusername. 

2️⃣ Your old username will become an alias, so you won’t lose access to it. You can still sign in and send and receive emails with both usernames. Plus, you can also revert to your old username.

3️⃣ You can choose a new username once a year — up to three times total. 

Here’s more information to get started -->https://goo.gle/4v3g1ry

media.alt: A minimalist graphic featuring an email address backspacing and  changing to a new gmail address.

update_data.content:
For Daniel, the path to his current job as Manager of Ads Marketing Analytics in Mexico City began with a passion for AI and a search for a culture where innovation is a fundamental part of the DNA.  He describes the Mexico City office as a “dream” because “it combines a world-class tech environment with the vibrant, fast-paced energy of one of the most beautiful cities in the world.”

Daniel’s day-to-day involves leading a team of data scientists and engineers to ensure every user data point serves a larger marketing purpose. His team uses “advanced analytics and cutting-edge AI to uncover key insights, optimize marketing campaigns and ensure our strategies are data-driven and highly effective.”

When it comes to joining the team, Daniel’s advice for future Googlers is to move beyond the classroom and start solving real problems. “Don’t just take classes; contribute to open-source projects, enter Kaggle competitions or solve a problem in your local community using data. Google looks for 'owners' — people who see a problem and don't wait for permission to fix it.”

Looking to join the Mexico City office? Check out our open roles →https://goo.gle/4qM2XDE

media.alt: Daniel sitting at a colorful table framed by a decorative Google-themed arch.
Daniel standing in front of a decorated "G" logo on an office wall.
Daniel sitting on a vibrant orange sofa in an office space.
Daniel standing in front of a large "G" mural on a wall.

Simra, a Google Recruiter in our NYC office, breaks down our interview process and shares her top tips on how to prepare. 

Explore our open roles →https://goo.gle/4rx4sWX…more

New month, new Gemini Drops ✨ See what's new in the Gemini app — from transferring your chat history from other AI apps to creating 3 minute tracks.

Learn more →gemini.google/gemini-drops

It’s AI Literacy Day and we’re making it easier than ever to use AI tools to reach your personal and professional goals. We developed the Google AI Professional Certificate to help you learn how to use generative AI at work — designed in collaboration with employers based on workplace skills they value most. It covers tips and tricks for using AI in areas like communication, research, data analysis, content generation, planning and organization, and vibe coding. 

Learn more about our AI training programs and get started today ↓https://goo.gle/3NSVgOy

media.alt: Abstract black background featuring orange and blue geometric curve; the Google Career Certificates logo is in the bottom right; there is text overlay “Get AI skills employers want” with a GIF of potential course options.

update_data.content:
